attack_summary: A data-theft/exfiltration attack on a compromised Ubuntu host in a small enterprise environment, involving local file discovery, staging, archiving, and outbound transfer over the network through a FortiGate perimeter.

mitigations.name: Perimeter firewall blocking of outbound exfiltration
mitigations.brief_description: Applied firewall rules to block the compromised host's outbound internet reachability and exfiltration traffic while trying to preserve LAN ICMP and essential web access.

LAN Connectivity Check (Ping).description: Checks inter-network connectivity by pinging a target host on the LAN. The target IP is defined by the fact 'lan.target.ip'.

Internet Connectivity Check (HTTP).description: Deadman ability that verifies internet connectivity by making an HTTP HEAD request to a well-known public endpoint. Uses curl instead of ping (ICMP) to test TCP-based network reachability. Returns the HTTP status code.

mitigations.name: Disable attacker tooling via PATH wrappers
mitigations.brief_description: Used PATH-precedence wrappers on the Ubuntu host to disrupt tools used for discovery, staging, archiving, and upload attempts, including find, tar, and curl.

mitigations.name: FortiGate egress filtering
mitigations.brief_description: Attempted to enforce outbound filtering on the FortiGate to block external exfiltration destinations while preserving normal connectivity.

Internet Connectivity Check (HTTP).description: Deadman ability that verifies internet connectivity by making an HTTP HEAD request to a well-known public endpoint. Uses curl instead of ping (ICMP) to test TCP-based network reachability. Returns the HTTP status code.

mitigations.name: Writable permission hardening on staging paths
mitigations.brief_description: Tightened permissions on attacker-controlled writable directories to prevent unauthorized staging and archive creation.

LAN Connectivity Check (Ping).description: Checks inter-network connectivity by pinging a target host on the LAN. The target IP is defined by the fact 'lan.target.ip'.

Internet Connectivity Check (HTTP).description: Deadman ability that verifies internet connectivity by making an HTTP HEAD request to a well-known public endpoint. Uses curl instead of ping (ICMP) to test TCP-based network reachability. Returns the HTTP status code.

mitigations.name: Broad host-specific perimeter egress policy
mitigations.brief_description: Configured a host-specific FortiGate egress policy intended to disrupt data transfer paths while preserving LAN ICMP and benign web traffic.

Internet Connectivity Check (HTTP).description: Deadman ability that verifies internet connectivity by making an HTTP HEAD request to a well-known public endpoint. Uses curl instead of ping (ICMP) to test TCP-based network reachability. Returns the HTTP status code.

mitigations.name: Application-layer egress filtering
mitigations.brief_description: Tried to restrict the compromised host to approved outbound web traffic patterns at the FortiGate while keeping required validation connectivity.

LAN Connectivity Check (Ping).description: Checks inter-network connectivity by pinging a target host on the LAN. The target IP is defined by the fact 'lan.target.ip'.

Internet Connectivity Check (HTTP).description: Deadman ability that verifies internet connectivity by making an HTTP HEAD request to a well-known public endpoint. Uses curl instead of ping (ICMP) to test TCP-based network reachability. Returns the HTTP status code.

mitigations.name: Remove execution rights from theft-chain utilities
mitigations.brief_description: Removed execute permissions from key utilities used in the theft workflow, including find, tar, curl, wget, nmap, nc.openbsd, socat, and hping3.

Internet Connectivity Check (HTTP).description: Deadman ability that verifies internet connectivity by making an HTTP HEAD request to a well-known public endpoint. Uses curl instead of ping (ICMP) to test TCP-based network reachability. Returns the HTTP status code.

mitigations.name: Tighten execution and access controls on local utilities
mitigations.brief_description: Applied additional host hardening to limit misuse of local tools for collection and archiving, but it did not reduce the attack's success rate.

LAN Connectivity Check (Ping).description: Checks inter-network connectivity by pinging a target host on the LAN. The target IP is defined by the fact 'lan.target.ip'.

Internet Connectivity Check (HTTP).description: Deadman ability that verifies internet connectivity by making an HTTP HEAD request to a well-known public endpoint. Uses curl instead of ping (ICMP) to test TCP-based network reachability. Returns the HTTP status code.

key_observations: The most effective mitigations were host-based disruption of attacker tooling, which reduced attack success from 1.0 to 0.33.
Network-level FortiGate egress controls were repeatedly attempted, but most failed to stop the exfiltration workflow despite preserving benign connectivity.
Several mitigations were operationally safe, meaning they preserved LAN ping and internet validation, but were still ineffective against the actual attack chain.
The attack relied on common local utilities for discovery, staging, compression, and upload, making application/tool restriction more effective than broad perimeter filtering.
overall_conclusion: The exfiltration attack was only reliably mitigated by disabling or constraining the compromised host's local tools used in the theft chain. Multiple perimeter filtering attempts preserved normal connectivity but did not block the attacker's workflow, showing that host-level control was the decisive defense in this session.
